mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index indicate immediate supply-side bottlenecks that disrupt manufacturing output and cause rapid negative S&P 500 price adjustments within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative drift during industrial production contractions, or the signal reverses direction in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicate restrictive real policy conditions that suppress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect fails to predict negative returns when inflation accelerates, or the combined signal yields positive returns in over 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer demand, which raises corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: Payroll growth periods produce negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of observations, or the rank correlation with forward returns drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts decline exceeding 10% signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related consumer spending, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 price movements within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of severe housing starts decline months, or the threshold effect disappears in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates an overheating economy where supply constraints outpace labor absorption, leading to inflation fears and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mechanism fails to hold when tested across different business cycle phases.
